name: KETSUYOUJUTSU: MA'ENDOU (BLOOD SORCERY: PATH OF THE FIENDFIRE)

description.value:
Type: Fuinjutsu

Rank: 6 (Spell Level 3)

Chakra Cost: 16

Learn DC: 21 | Successes: 6

Perform: 9 | Perform DC: 23

Components: C, H, M

This technique can only be used on a fire elemental that would grant you XP when defeated, whose current HP is below half its maximum and whose CP is higher than 30. You seal the target's essence in a special mixture, which must be consumed within 3 rounds. When consumed, you suffer 5 negative levels for 1 week, and are exhausted while they last, in addition to suffering from a blazing fever that causes a -4 penalty to Intelligence-, Wisdom- and Charisma-based checks, as well as Concentration checks, and you are unable to think clearly. Once the exhaustion clears, so does the fever. In addition, you are unable to take any action for 1d4 minutes after consuming the mixture as searing pain cripples your mind and body.

Once all the effects above subside, you lose all elemental affinities, gain the Ignis Vitae subtype (p345) and can no longer gain elemental affinities from any source other than the Ignis Vitae subtype.

Up to four empty void seals (from Ketsuyoujutsu: Kuuin) can be used to absorb the shock, reducing the negative levels by 1 per empty seal. Any creature that isn't you can consume the mixture, but doing so has no effects (other than ingesting an exceptionally awfultasting gruel)

This technique, leads to Path of the Fiendfire, an offensive branch of Blood Sorcery which grants access to powerful techniques (p346).
